tool_input.summary: Found Juno/PDS datasets that can be used to derive radial profiles of electron/ion (proton + heavy-ion) number density and pressure on the dawnside magnetodisk. Core products are JADE plasma moments (thermal) plus Juno Waves electron density (independent ne) and optional JEDI energetic particle data for suprathermal/energetic pressure. Use Juno SPICE kernels to compute spacecraft radial distance and local time (dawnside selection), and MAG/FGM to identify magnetodisk/current-sheet intervals if needed. Note: in this catalog, JADE and JEDI products appear as PDS3 volumes; a PDS4 JADE/JEDI moments archive was not returned by search.

candidates.title: JNO J JAD 5 MOMENTS V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Primary Juno/JADE “moments” product; intended for plasma number density and moment-derived quantities (used to compute thermal pressure) versus time. Combine with SPICE to bin by radial distance and select dawn local times.

candidates.title: JNO J SW JAD 5 CALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated JADE solar-wind/plasma distributions that can be used when you need to re-derive moments (e.g., species-separated protons vs heavy ions) or validate pressure computations beyond the precomputed moments.

candidates.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities from Characteristic Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Derived electron number density (ne) from Waves characteristic plasma frequencies; useful to track electron density vs radial distance and cross-check JADE-derived densities in the magnetodisk.

candidates.title: JNO E J SS WAV 3 CDR BSTFULL V2.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Waves calibrated data (burst/full). If you need to (re)derive electron density from plasma wave features rather than using the derived PDS4 electron-density bundle.

candidates.title: JNO J JED 3 CDR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Juno/JEDI calibrated energetic particle data; useful for estimating suprathermal/energetic contributions to proton and heavy-ion pressure in the magnetodisk (complementing JADE thermal pressure).

candidates.title: JNO J 3 FGM CAL V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Juno/MAG (FGM) calibrated magnetic field data; helps identify magnetodisk/current-sheet crossings and provides B for pressure balance/context while binning plasma parameters by radial distance on the dawn flank.

candidates.title: JUNO SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: NAIF SPICE kernels for Juno geometry. Required to compute spacecraft position (radial distance in RJ), magnetic local time (dawnside selection), and coordinate transforms needed to build radial profiles from JADE/Waves/JEDI time series.

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
How do the number density and pressure of electrons, protons, and heavy ions change with radial distance in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk as observed by Juno?
